title: Fair-Use Cache Keys, TTL, Clock Injection, Copies, and Eviction · Execution Trace: Pokedex Go Ttl Clock
summary: The cache keys raw commands, uses time.Now deep inside methods, returns mutable shared slices, never expires failures, and grows without a capacity policy. Predict the user decision, Go ownership, command state, API contract, resource budget, failure, and observable evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the pokedex go ttl clock increment in bounded TTL resource cache with observable decisions. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, change…
objectives: Inject a clock, define freshness at exact boundaries, and distinguish fetched, stored, observed, and expires times.
Canonicalize endpoint, resource identity, and representation into a typed cache key without mixing user spelling or presentation.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Pokedex product engineering and Go Predict Pokedex Go Ttl Clock · Theory Pokedex Go Ttl Clock
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Go team handles case 4972 during an accessible regional field-guide session. Build deterministic Go evidence to reconcile canonical cache decisions, freshness, ownership, and capacity eviction; all goroutines, bodies, signals, timers, and files require named cleanup ownership; then change one catch, owned record, encounter relation, or history state and prove the prior invariant. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original fixtures only. Real PokéAPI traffic, DNS, sockets, TLS, host files, terminals, signals, race and leak claims, load, cross-platform packaging, accessibility review, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.
